AC: tag command prints {embedder:{tag:score}} top-k per embedder, recomputed each run; candidate domain = 1/2/3-grams mined from corpus; per-embedder cosine top-k; tags not persisted durably; refresh fn shared by search and tag; tests cover mining, per-embedder top-k, non-persistence

Body.raw:
Goal: add a top-level `tags` field to todos, shape {embedder: {tag: score}},
top-k (~3) tags per embedder. Zero-shot semantic tagging over a candidate
domain of 1/2/3-grams ("n-phrases") mined from the whole corpus (Summary+Body
raw of every todo).

Design (from /tmp/foo):
- Candidate domain: 1,2,3-grams (n-phrases; split on phrase separators:
  sentences, headings, ascii art) across the whole corpus. Pruning is a TODO
  and may not be needed. Linguistic pruning dropped for now (must stay
  programmatic, no agent intervention).
- For each (todo doc-embedding x candidate tag) compute cosine; keep k highest
  per embedder. Scores from different embedders are NOT comparable, so each
  embedder gets its own top-k set.
- Tags are NOT persisted durably: recomputed each run (along with the embedding
  refresh). The field is a computed/merged view produced at tag/read time, not
  written back to the store.
- Entry points: a new `tag` command, and wired into `doctor`. Default embedder
  set = all available (as search does); availability comes from the entry point.

Reuse / prerequisites:
- Shared refresh function: extract the lazy vector backfill currently inside
  search (todo.py ~1132-1143) into an internal "refresh embeddings for all
  todos" used by BOTH search and tag. Trust existing vectors; recompute only
  empties. Field edits already zero their vectors (_strip_field_vectors on
  changed raw).
- n-phrase chunking: today each field is ONE chunk (todo.py:476, "until
  chunking lands the whole field is one chunk"). foo "Managing embeddings"
  wants 1,2,3 n-phrase chunking for Summary/Body. SCOPE QUESTION: do chunking
  in this todo, or ship tags against whole-field vectors first and treat
  chunking as a sibling todo.
- Stored chunks are bare vectors with no source text, so candidate tag
  embeddings must be computed fresh per embedder (cannot reverse-map a chunk
  vector back to its tag string).

Touch points: todo.py (embed helpers ~355-520; search/refresh ~1085-1170;
doctor cmd; new `tag` cmd + argparse), todo_embed.py (embedders/cosine),
todo_store.py + todo_db.py (read-only: corpus text + stored vectors). Tests:
test_todo.py, test_todo_embed*.py.

Open decisions (confirm before init + implementation):
1. Scope: include n-phrase chunking here, or tags-only against current
   whole-field vectors (chunking as a sibling todo)?
2. Isolation: repo jovlinger/utils is on master with uncommitted WIP (M todo.py,
   SKILL.md, ...). Need a worktree/branch strategy that does not entangle it.

Summary.raw: Zero-shot semantic tagging: top-level tags field {embedder:{tag:score}}
